evals.prompt: I've been trying for about 2 hours to get this FastAPI API endpoint to stop returning 500 when I send it a payload with a nested field. I've tried validating the schema with Pydantic, checking server logs, and even reinstalled dependencies. The error says something about `TypeError: 'NoneType' object is not subscriptable` but I can't find where the None is coming from. The file is at `src/api/routes/users.py`.
evals.expected_output: Identifies the bug in user.address without null check and provides a concrete solution

assertions.name: identifies_bug_location
assertions.description: Identifies the exact line where the bug occurs

evals.prompt: I'm setting up a new personal project and can't decide between SQLite or PostgreSQL. It's a dashboard to track my expenses, probably around 10-20 tables, no more than 50k rows total, but I want it to be easy to deploy on a cheap VPS. What would you recommend?
evals.expected_output: Gives a clear recommendation with technical reasoning based on the user's constraints

assertions.name: considers_constraints
assertions.description: Mentions at least 2 of the user's constraints

assertions.name: provides_reasoning
assertions.description: Explains the why with technical arguments

evals.prompt: I need to implement a parser for a custom log format that has lines with irregular timestamps, indented blocks, and sometimes breaks in the middle. I've never done anything like this and don't want to do it with regex spaghetti. How would you approach it?
evals.expected_output: Proposes a structured approach (not regex spaghetti) that handles irregular timestamps and broken lines

assertions.name: avoids_regex_spaghetti
assertions.description: Doesn't propose a single giant regex

assertions.name: handles_irregular_timestamps
assertions.description: Mentions how to handle irregular timestamps

assertions.name: handles_broken_lines
assertions.description: Proposes a strategy for broken lines
